steps: Log in to your self-hosted Strapi admin panel (typically at http://localhost:1337/admin or your deployment URL)
Go to Settings > Global settings > API Tokens
Enter a name and optional description for the token
Select the token duration (unlimited, 7 days, 30 days, or 90 days)
Select the token type: Read-only, Full access, or Custom
For Custom type, expand each content type and check the specific permissions you want
Click 'Save' and copy the token immediately — it is shown only once (unless an encryption key is configured)

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Token
credential_fields.description: Your Strapi API token generated in the admin panel under Settings > API Tokens

notes: Strapi is self-hosted — the base URL depends on your deployment, not api.strapi.io
Free and open-source; no SaaS signup required
Token is shown only once unless admin.secrets.encryptionKey is set in your Strapi config
Token types: Read-only (GET only), Full access (all methods), Custom (granular per content type)
Token duration can be set to unlimited or time-limited for security

test_endpoint.description: List available content types to verify the API token works
